title: 15 Net income or loss from business 2020
description: Complete question 15 to report income you derive or losses you incur from any business.
keywords: TaxPack, tax return 2020, supplement, income, net income, loss, income from business, loss from business, net loss, non-commercial, 2020, paper tax return, paper form, paper return, paper lodgement

content_blocks.text: Did you derive income or incur a loss from any business in 2019–20?

rows: Go to question 16 Deferred non-commercial business losses , or return to main menu Individual tax return instructions 2020 .

You should lodge your tax return using myTax or a registered tax agent. If you are unable to use myTax or a registered tax agent, contact us on 13 28 66 and we will mail you a paper tax return and Business and Professional items schedule.

content_blocks.text: This question covers:

items: income from being a sole trader
income or a loss from a primary production business
income under a pay as you go (PAYG) voluntary agreement
income from which an amount was withheld because you did not quote your Australian business number (ABN)
income you derived as a foreign resident from which an amount was withheld because it was subject to foreign resident withholding
income of an independent contractor working under a labour hire arrangement

income from the following specified payments payment for tutorial services provided under the Indigenous Student Success Programme (formerly known as the Indigenous Tutorial Assistance Scheme) of the Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet payment for translation and interpretation services provided for the Translating and Interpreting Service National of the Department of Home Affairs income as a performing artist in a promotional activity
any other business income
the small business income tax offset (see Part D ).

content_blocks.text: A specified payment is a payment specified in tax law for PAYG withholding purposes.
Do not show at this item:

items: personal services income included at item 14 Personal services income (PSI) 2020
foreign source business income included at item 20 Foreign source income
credit for amounts withheld from business as a result of the foreign resident capital gains withholding rules. Show the amounts of credit at item 18 Capital gains .

content_blocks.text: You need to know
Before you complete this item, see Business and professional items 2020 .
You must show your net income or loss (that is, gross business income less business deductions) at item 15 , B for primary production and C for non-primary production.
You carry on a primary production business if you carry on a business undertaking:

items: plant or animal cultivation (or both)
fishing or pearling (or both)
tree farming or felling (or both).

content_blocks.text: See also:

items: Information for primary producers 2020

content_blocks.text: What you may need
These publications may help you to correctly complete this item:

items: Taxation Ruling TR 92/18 Income tax: bad debts
Taxation Ruling TR 93/30 Income tax: deductions for home office expenses
Taxation Ruling TR 96/7 Income tax: record keeping – section 262A – general principles
Taxation Ruling TR 96/11 Income tax: record keeping – taxi industry – guidelines for recording taxi takings
Taxation Ruling TR 97/11 Income tax: am I carrying on a business of primary production?
Taxation Ruling TR 97/23 Income tax: deductions for repairs
Taxation Ruling TR 2018/2 Income tax: record keeping and access – electronic records
Guide to depreciating assets 2020
Small business entity concessions

content_blocks.text: Completing this item
Part A
Business and professional items schedule for individuals 2020 and Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020
Step 1
Complete the Business and professional items section.
Step 2
If you received business income from which tax was withheld, complete the Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020 .
Part B
Did you derive income or incur a loss from a primary production business?
Step 1
Transfer your net income or loss from a primary production business shown at Y item P8 in the Business and professional items section to B item 15 . Do not show cents.
Step 2
If you made a loss, print L in the LOSS box at the right of B item 15 .
Step 3
If you made a loss from one or more business activities, check that you have completed items P3 and P9 in the Business and professional items section.
Part C
Did you derive income or incur a loss from any non-primary production business?
Step 1
Transfer your net income or loss from a non-primary production business shown at Z item P8 in the Business and professional items section to C item 15 . Do not show cents.
Step 2
If you made a loss, print L in the LOSS box at the right of C .
Step 3
If you made a loss from one or more business activities, check that you have completed items P3 and P9 in the Business and professional items section.

If you carried on a business as an author of a literary, dramatic, musical or artistic work or as an inventor, performing artist, production associate or active sportsperson, you must also write the amount of income from these business activities at Z item 24 Other income 2020 . You will not be taxed twice on this income.
Part D
Are you a small business entity?

You may be entitled to the small business income tax offset of up to $1,000. We will use the information you provide here to work out the amount of your tax offset. The amount you show at this question is not included in your income.

To answer Part D, you need to work out your net small business income, which is your assessable income from carrying on your business less your deductions to the extent that they are attributable to that assessable income. For more information, see Small business income tax offset .
Do not show at Part D:

items: personal services income included at item 14 Personal services income (PSI) 2020
salary, wages or directors fees
dividend income of directors
dividend and interest income, unless it is derived from carrying on the business activity and you have shown an amount at 15 Net income or loss from business 2020
partnership share of net small business income less deductions attributable to that share shown at D item 13
trust share of net small business income less deductions attributable to that share shown at E item 13 .

content_blocks.text: If you have net small business income from P8 of Business and professional items section you completed, go to Step 1.

If your only income or loss from carrying on a business as a small business entity is at M item 20 , then to work out your net small business income use our Small business income tax offset calculator .
Completing this item
Step 1
If you have net small business income from P8 of Business and professional items show it at A item 15 Net small business income then go to Part E.
Step 2

If your only income or loss from carrying on a business as a small business entity is at M item 20 and you used our Small business income tax offset calculator to work out your net small business income, then show the result at A item 15 Net small business income .
Part E

In your calculation of total business income in the Business and professional items section, did you include income from which an amount of tax was withheld at the INCOME items B, C, D, E, F, N or O item P8?

rows: Go to Check that you have...

content_blocks.text: Step 1
Did you have any amounts of tax withheld under a pay as you go (PAYG) voluntary agreement?

Add up all the amounts on your Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020 shown in the Tax withheld boxes where you have printed V in the Type box. These amounts must correspond with the Gross payments shown at E and F item P8 in the Business and professional items section. Show this total at D item 15 . Do not show cents.
Step 2
Did you have any amounts of tax withheld because you did not quote your Australian business number (ABN)?

Add up all the amounts on your Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020 shown in the Tax withheld boxes where you have printed N in the Type box. These amounts must correspond with the Gross payments shown at C and D item P8 in the Business and professional items section. Show this total at W item 15 . Show cents.
Step 3
Did you, a foreign resident, have any amounts of tax withheld because your income was subject to foreign resident withholding?

Add up all the amounts on your Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020 shown in the Tax withheld boxes where you have printed F in the Type box. These amounts must correspond with the Gross payments shown at B item P8 in the Business and professional items section. Show this amount at E item 15 . Show cents.
Do not include credits for amounts withheld as a result of the foreign resident capital gains withholding rules at E item 15 .
Step 4
Did you have any amounts of tax withheld because you received income:

items: working under a labour hire arrangement, or
from a specified payment?

rows: Go to Check that you have...

content_blocks.text: Add up all the amounts on your Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020 shown in the Tax withheld boxes where you have printed S in the Type box. These amounts must correspond with the Gross payments shown at N and O item P8 in the Business and professional items section. Show this amount at F item 15 . Do not show cents.
Check that you have...

items: read Business and professional items 2020
completed the Business and professional items section

transferred the amounts from Y and Z item P8 in the Business and professional items section to B and C item 15 respectively on your tax return (if these amounts are losses then check that you have printed L in the Loss boxes at the right of B and C )
completed A item 15 if you are a small business entity.

content_blocks.text: If you received business income from which tax was withheld, check that you have:

items: completed the Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020 and attached it to page 3 of your tax return
written amounts at D , W , E and F item 15 , if required
not included the foreign resident capital gains withholding credit at E item 15 .

content_blocks.text: Where to go next

items: Go to question 16 Deferred non-commercial business losses 2020 .
Return to main menu Individual tax return instructions 2020 .
Go back to question 14 Personal services income (PSI) 2020 .

content_text:
Did you derive income or incur a loss from any business in 2019–20?
No | Go to question 16 Deferred non-commercial business losses , or return to main menu Individual tax return instructions 2020 .
Yes | You should lodge your tax return using myTax or a registered tax agent. If you are unable to use myTax or a registered tax agent, contact us on 13 28 66 and we will mail you a paper tax return and Business and Professional items schedule.
This question covers:
- income from being a sole trader
- income or a loss from a primary production business
- income under a pay as you go (PAYG) voluntary agreement
- income from which an amount was withheld because you did not quote your Australian business number (ABN)
- income you derived as a foreign resident from which an amount was withheld because it was subject to foreign resident withholding
- income of an independent contractor working under a labour hire arrangement
- income from the following specified payments payment for tutorial services provided under the Indigenous Student Success Programme (formerly known as the Indigenous Tutorial Assistance Scheme) of the Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet payment for translation and interpretation services provided for the Translating and Interpreting Service National of the Department of Home Affairs income as a performing artist in a promotional activity
- any other business income
- the small business income tax offset (see Part D ).
A specified payment is a payment specified in tax law for PAYG withholding purposes.
Do not show at this item:
- personal services income included at item 14 Personal services income (PSI) 2020
- foreign source business income included at item 20 Foreign source income
- credit for amounts withheld from business as a result of the foreign resident capital gains withholding rules. Show the amounts of credit at item 18 Capital gains .
## You need to know
Before you complete this item, see Business and professional items 2020 .
You must show your net income or loss (that is, gross business income less business deductions) at item 15 , B for primary production and C for non-primary production.
You carry on a primary production business if you carry on a business undertaking:
- plant or animal cultivation (or both)
- fishing or pearling (or both)
- tree farming or felling (or both).
See also:
- Information for primary producers 2020
## What you may need
These publications may help you to correctly complete this item:
- Taxation Ruling TR 92/18 Income tax: bad debts
- Taxation Ruling TR 93/30 Income tax: deductions for home office expenses
- Taxation Ruling TR 96/7 Income tax: record keeping – section 262A – general principles
- Taxation Ruling TR 96/11 Income tax: record keeping – taxi industry – guidelines for recording taxi takings
- Taxation Ruling TR 97/11 Income tax: am I carrying on a business of primary production?
- Taxation Ruling TR 97/23 Income tax: deductions for repairs
- Taxation Ruling TR 2018/2 Income tax: record keeping and access – electronic records
- Guide to depreciating assets 2020
- Small business entity concessions
## Completing this item
### Part A
Business and professional items schedule for individuals 2020 and Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020
#### Step 1
Complete the Business and professional items section.
#### Step 2
If you received business income from which tax was withheld, complete the Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020 .
### Part B
Did you derive income or incur a loss from a primary production business?
No | Go to Part C .
Yes | Read on.
#### Step 1
Transfer your net income or loss from a primary production business shown at Y item P8 in the Business and professional items section to B item 15 . Do not show cents.
#### Step 2
If you made a loss, print L in the LOSS box at the right of B item 15 .
#### Step 3
If you made a loss from one or more business activities, check that you have completed items P3 and P9 in the Business and professional items section.
### Part C
Did you derive income or incur a loss from any non-primary production business?
No | Go to Part D .
Yes | Read on.
#### Step 1
Transfer your net income or loss from a non-primary production business shown at Z item P8 in the Business and professional items section to C item 15 . Do not show cents.
#### Step 2
If you made a loss, print L in the LOSS box at the right of C .
#### Step 3
If you made a loss from one or more business activities, check that you have completed items P3 and P9 in the Business and professional items section.
If you carried on a business as an author of a literary, dramatic, musical or artistic work or as an inventor, performing artist, production associate or active sportsperson, you must also write the amount of income from these business activities at Z item 24 Other income 2020 . You will not be taxed twice on this income.
### Part D
Are you a small business entity?
No | Go to Part E .
Yes | Read on.
You may be entitled to the small business income tax offset of up to $1,000. We will use the information you provide here to work out the amount of your tax offset. The amount you show at this question is not included in your income.
To answer Part D, you need to work out your net small business income, which is your assessable income from carrying on your business less your deductions to the extent that they are attributable to that assessable income. For more information, see Small business income tax offset .
Do not show at Part D:
- personal services income included at item 14 Personal services income (PSI) 2020
- salary, wages or directors fees
- dividend income of directors
- dividend and interest income, unless it is derived from carrying on the business activity and you have shown an amount at 15 Net income or loss from business 2020
- partnership share of net small business income less deductions attributable to that share shown at D item 13
- trust share of net small business income less deductions attributable to that share shown at E item 13 .
If you have net small business income from P8 of Business and professional items section you completed, go to Step 1.
If your only income or loss from carrying on a business as a small business entity is at M item 20 , then to work out your net small business income use our Small business income tax offset calculator .
## Completing this item
### Step 1
If you have net small business income from P8 of Business and professional items show it at A item 15 Net small business income then go to Part E.
### Step 2
If your only income or loss from carrying on a business as a small business entity is at M item 20 and you used our Small business income tax offset calculator to work out your net small business income, then show the result at A item 15 Net small business income .
### Part E
In your calculation of total business income in the Business and professional items section, did you include income from which an amount of tax was withheld at the INCOME items B, C, D, E, F, N or O item P8?
No | Go to Check that you have...
Yes | Read on.
#### Step 1
Did you have any amounts of tax withheld under a pay as you go (PAYG) voluntary agreement?
No | Go to Step 2 .
Yes | Read on.
Add up all the amounts on your Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020 shown in the Tax withheld boxes where you have printed V in the Type box. These amounts must correspond with the Gross payments shown at E and F item P8 in the Business and professional items section. Show this total at D item 15 . Do not show cents.
#### Step 2
Did you have any amounts of tax withheld because you did not quote your Australian business number (ABN)?
No | Go to Step 3 .
Yes | Read on.
Add up all the amounts on your Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020 shown in the Tax withheld boxes where you have printed N in the Type box. These amounts must correspond with the Gross payments shown at C and D item P8 in the Business and professional items section. Show this total at W item 15 . Show cents.
#### Step 3
Did you, a foreign resident, have any amounts of tax withheld because your income was subject to foreign resident withholding?
No | Go to Step 4 .
Yes | Read on.
Add up all the amounts on your Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020 shown in the Tax withheld boxes where you have printed F in the Type box. These amounts must correspond with the Gross payments shown at B item P8 in the Business and professional items section. Show this amount at E item 15 . Show cents.
Do not include credits for amounts withheld as a result of the foreign resident capital gains withholding rules at E item 15 .
#### Step 4
Did you have any amounts of tax withheld because you received income:
- working under a labour hire arrangement, or
- from a specified payment?
No | Go to Check that you have...
Yes | Read on.
Add up all the amounts on your Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020 shown in the Tax withheld boxes where you have printed S in the Type box. These amounts must correspond with the Gross payments shown at N and O item P8 in the Business and professional items section. Show this amount at F item 15 . Do not show cents.
## Check that you have...
- read Business and professional items 2020
- completed the Business and professional items section
- transferred the amounts from Y and Z item P8 in the Business and professional items section to B and C item 15 respectively on your tax return (if these amounts are losses then check that you have printed L in the Loss boxes at the right of B and C )
- completed A item 15 if you are a small business entity.
If you received business income from which tax was withheld, check that you have:
- completed the Individual PAYG payment summary schedule 2020 and attached it to page 3 of your tax return
- written amounts at D , W , E and F item 15 , if required
- not included the foreign resident capital gains withholding credit at E item 15 .
## Where to go next
- Go to question 16 Deferred non-commercial business losses 2020 .
- Return to main menu Individual tax return instructions 2020 .
- Go back to question 14 Personal services income (PSI) 2020 .
